heading: Use of photovoltaic energy in public buildings
label: 3177 Use of photovoltaic energy in public buildings
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (b)(1), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 109–58Aug. 8, 2005

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator of General Services may establish a photovoltaic energy commercialization program for the procurement and installation of photovoltaic solar electric systems for electric production in new and existing public buildings.

children.label: A
children.text: To accelerate the growth of a commercially viable photovoltaic industry to make this energy system available to the general public as an option which can reduce the national consumption of fossil fuel.

children.label: B
children.text: To reduce the fossil fuel consumption and costs of the Federal Government.

children.label: C
children.text: To attain the goal of installing solar energy systems in 20,000 Federal buildings by 2010, as contained in the Federal Government’s Million Solar Roof Initiative of 1997.

children.label: D
children.text: To stimulate the general use within the Federal Government of life-cycle costing and innovative procurement methods.

children.label: E
children.text: To develop program performance data to support policy decisions on future incentive programs with respect to energy.

children.heading: Acquisition of photovoltaic solar electric systems.—
children.label: 3 Acquisition of photovoltaic solar electric systems.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The program shall provide for the acquisition of photovoltaic solar electric systems and associated storage capability for use in public buildings.

children.heading: .—Acquisition levels
children.label: B .—Acquisition levels
children.text: The acquisition of photovoltaic electric systems shall be at a level substantial enough to allow use of low-cost production techniques with at least 150 megawatts (peak) cumulative acquired during the 5 years of the program.

children.label: A
children.text: issue such rules and regulations as may be appropriate to monitor and assess the performance and operation of photovoltaic solar electric systems installed pursuant to this subsection;

children.label: B
children.text: develop innovative procurement strategies for the acquisition of such systems; and

children.label: C
children.text: transmit to Congress an annual report on the results of the program.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of enactment of this section, the Administrator shall establish a photovoltaic solar energy systems evaluation program to evaluate such photovoltaic solar energy systems as are required in public buildings.

children.heading: .—Program requirement
children.label: 2 .—Program requirement
children.text: In evaluating photovoltaic solar energy systems under the program, the Administrator shall ensure that such systems reflect the most advanced technology.

children.heading: .—Photovoltaic energy commercialization program
children.label: 1 .—Photovoltaic energy commercialization program
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out subsection (a) $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2006 through 2010. Such sums shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: .—Photovoltaic systems evaluation program
children.label: 2 .—Photovoltaic systems evaluation program
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out subsection (b) $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2006 through 2010. Such sums shall remain available until expended.
